economic_notes: Consolidated entity includes convenience stores, gas bars, hotel, casino, resort (Stoney Nakoda Resort), holdings company, telecom, oil and gas, contracting, food bank, and multiple settlement trusts. COVID-19 pandemic noted as creating significant uncertainty. Flood disaster clean-up costs recovery from Province of Alberta also noted.
notes: Stoney First Nation is a large consolidated entity comprising three First Nations (Bearspaw, Chiniki, Wesley) and approximately 45 subsidiary entities including businesses, trusts, and program entities. One government business partnership (Chiniki Trico Limited Partnership) accounted for by modified equity method. Large trust funds held by federal government totalling $166.8M (Heritage Royalty Trust). Settlement trusts of $19.6M also held. Significant deferred revenue balance of $36.9M. COVID-19 pandemic uncertainty noted extensively. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. Total government transfers estimated as ISC recognized ($47,536,649) + Health Canada recognized ($13,132,531) + HRD recognized ($1,897,570) + Special Child Allowance recognized ($90,845) + CMHC ($829,807) + Provincial/GoA recognized ($13,732,733) + FNDF recognized ($3,311,722) + Band funds recognized ($5,359,513) = ~$85,891,370 but using revenue line amounts directly: ISC Fixed + ISC Set + CMHC + HRD + Health Canada + Capital band funds + Revenue band funds + Provincial Funding + FNDF + Special Child Allowance = 46873394+663255+829807+1897571+13127455+1087615+4271899+12052512+3311722+90845 = 84206075; OSR = 129474773 - 84206075 = 45268698. Values reflect fiscal year ended March 31, 2020.
